questions.text: A customer at your bank branch in Addis Ababa is upset because a wire transfer to their family in Hawassa has been delayed for three days. They raise their voice and say, 'This is unacceptable! My family needs this money now!' What is the best first response?

options.text: Tell them to calm down and wait like everyone else
Acknowledge their frustration, apologize for the delay, and immediately check the transfer status in the system
Explain that delays are normal and there is nothing you can do
Ask them to come back tomorrow when the manager is available

questions.text: You work at an Ethio Telecom service center. A customer arrives five minutes before closing time with a complex SIM replacement issue. What is the most professional approach?

options.text: Tell them to come back first thing tomorrow morning
Quickly process what you can and ask them to return for the rest
Serve the customer fully, even if it means staying a few minutes past closing time
Direct them to the call center hotline instead

questions.text: A hotel guest at your front desk in Bahir Dar asks a question in English, but their accent makes it difficult to understand. What should you do?

options.text: Pretend you understood and give a general answer
Ask a colleague who might understand better to take over
Politely ask them to repeat or rephrase, and confirm your understanding by paraphrasing back to them
Hand them a pen and paper and ask them to write it down

questions.text: Two customers at your retail store begin arguing over who was next in line. The situation is escalating. What is the best course of action?

options.text: Ignore the argument and continue serving the current customer
Tell both customers to stop arguing or leave the store
Calmly intervene, acknowledge both customers, and suggest serving them in the order you observed while offering a sincere apology for any confusion
Ask other customers in line to decide who was first

questions.text: You are a call center agent for a delivery company. A customer calls saying their package was marked as delivered but they never received it. What should you do first?

options.text: Tell them to check with their neighbors
Apologize, verify the delivery details in your system, and initiate a trace on the package while keeping the customer informed of each step
Suggest they file a police report for stolen property
Tell them the delivery driver confirmed it was delivered so there is nothing more you can do

questions.text: A regular customer at your coffee shop in Bole always complains about small things, but continues to come back daily. Today they complain the macchiato is too hot. What is the right mindset?

options.text: This customer is impossible to please, so just nod and move on
Every complaint is an opportunity to improve, and loyal customers who voice concerns are more valuable than those who silently leave
Tell them politely that coffee is supposed to be served hot
Start making their drink lukewarm from now on without asking

questions.text: You work at an Ethiopian Airlines counter. A passenger is confused about baggage allowance and becomes frustrated when you explain the policy. Which communication technique is most effective?

options.text: Repeat the same policy explanation but louder and slower
Print out the full terms and conditions and hand it to them
Use simple language, give a specific example relevant to their situation, and offer to help them repack or explore alternatives
Refer them to the airline's website for full details

questions.text: A customer at your electronics store demands a full refund for a phone purchased three months ago, claiming it is defective. Your store policy only allows returns within 30 days. How do you handle this?

options.text: Refuse the refund immediately and quote the policy
Give the full refund to avoid conflict
Empathize with their frustration, clearly explain the return policy, then explore alternatives such as warranty repair, manufacturer support, or a store credit as a goodwill gesture
Tell them to contact the phone manufacturer directly

questions.text: You are working at a hotel reception in Lalibela during peak tourist season. A guest with a confirmed reservation arrives, but due to overbooking, no rooms are available. What should you do?

options.text: Apologize and suggest they find another hotel on their own
Sincerely apologize, immediately arrange equivalent or better accommodation at a nearby partner hotel at no extra cost, and offer complimentary transport and a future stay discount
Offer them a seat in the lobby until a room becomes available
Blame the booking system and tell them it is not your fault

questions.text: Your supervisor at the bank instructs you to upsell a new savings account to every customer. An elderly customer comes in only to check their balance. What is the most ethical approach?

options.text: Skip the upsell because they are elderly and probably would not understand
Aggressively pitch the product because your supervisor told you to
Help them with their balance inquiry first, then briefly mention the savings account only if it genuinely fits their needs, without pressure
Have a colleague handle the upsell so it does not look pushy coming from you

questions.text: A customer calls your telecom call center and begins explaining their issue, but they are speaking very fast and jumping between topics. What should you do?

options.text: Interrupt them and ask them to slow down
Listen patiently, take notes, then summarize what you heard and ask them to confirm before proceeding
Just address the first issue they mentioned and hope the rest resolves itself
Put them on hold while you figure out what they need

questions.text: A customer at your bank branch accuses you personally of stealing money from their account. You know this is not true and the discrepancy is due to a system fee. How do you respond?

options.text: Become defensive and tell them the accusation is offensive
Stay calm, do not take it personally, show them the account statement with the fee breakdown, and explain each charge clearly
Call security because the customer is being abusive
Refuse to serve them until they apologize

questions.text: You work at an Ethiopian Airlines check-in counter. A family of five arrives late for their flight. The gate is about to close and they are panicking. What is the best action?

options.text: Tell them the flight is closed and they should rebook
Quickly assess if boarding is still possible, contact the gate agent immediately, expedite their check-in, and clearly communicate what they need to do to make it
Let them skip the security line since they are late
Blame them for arriving late and process rebooking at full fare

questions.text: You have been serving customers nonstop for six hours without a break. You are exhausted. The next customer in line has a complex issue. What is the correct professional response?

options.text: Rush through the interaction so you can take your break sooner
Tell the customer you are tired and ask them to be brief
Serve the customer with the same quality and attention as the first customer of the day, and request your break afterward
Transfer them to a colleague so you can rest

questions.text: A customer at your retail store asks about a product you are not familiar with. What is the best response?

options.text: Make up an answer based on what seems logical
Tell them you do not know and suggest they look it up online
Honestly say you are not certain, but offer to find the accurate information from a colleague or product documentation right away
Redirect them to a different product you know more about

questions.text: A customer at your Ethio Telecom office is furious because they were charged for a data bundle they did not subscribe to. They threaten to go to the media. What is the most effective approach?

options.text: Tell them going to the media will not change anything
Immediately refund the charge without investigating
Take their concern seriously, investigate the charge in front of them, explain what happened, process a correction if warranted, and document the issue for follow-up
Escalate to your manager and step away from the situation

questions.text: You are a teller at a Commercial Bank of Ethiopia branch. An elderly customer cannot remember their PIN and has no ID with them. They insist you should recognize them since they have been banking there for 20 years. What do you do?

options.text: Process their request since you do recognize them from previous visits
Empathetically explain that security procedures protect their account, suggest they return with ID, and offer to help them through the PIN reset process
Call the police because this could be fraud
Ask nearby customers to vouch for the person's identity

questions.text: A colleague at your call center frequently gives customers incorrect information, leading to repeated callbacks. What should you do?

options.text: Ignore it since it is not your responsibility
Correct the misinformation when those customers call back and report the pattern to your supervisor privately and respectfully
Confront your colleague publicly during a shift
Tell customers that your colleague is unreliable

questions.text: You work at a hotel in Adama. A foreign guest is trying to communicate a dietary restriction but there is a significant language barrier. What is the best approach?

options.text: Nod and assure them everything will be fine
Use a translation app, visual menus, or involve a multilingual staff member to ensure you fully understand their needs before confirming
Serve them a standard meal and hope for the best
Tell them you only serve Ethiopian food and there are no alternatives

questions.text: A customer at your retail shop claims they were given the wrong change and accuses you of cheating them. You are confident the change was correct. What should you do?

options.text: Firmly tell them the change was correct and refuse to discuss further
Give them extra money to end the argument
Remain calm, offer to count the register together, show the receipt, and resolve the discrepancy transparently
Call your manager immediately without trying to resolve it yourself

questions.text: You work at a Dashen Bank branch. The network system goes down during peak hours and a long queue of impatient customers is forming. What is the most appropriate response?

options.text: Close the branch until the system comes back
Proactively inform waiting customers about the issue, provide a realistic time estimate, offer water, suggest alternatives like mobile banking, and process what you can manually
Keep quiet and hope the system comes back quickly
Tell customers to go to another branch

questions.text: A customer at your store makes a casual but mildly offensive joke about your appearance. What is the most professional response?

options.text: Laugh along even though you feel uncomfortable
Make a similar joke back at them
Maintain your composure, do not engage with the comment, and redirect the conversation to how you can assist them
Tell them their comment is inappropriate and refuse to serve them

questions.text: You are explaining a new mobile banking feature to a customer who is not tech-savvy. They seem confused after your explanation. What should you do?

options.text: Repeat the same explanation, assuming they will get it the second time
Tell them to ask a younger family member to help them
Demonstrate the feature step by step on their phone with their permission, using simple language and checking their understanding at each step
Give them the customer service number to call later for help

questions.text: A VIP customer at your hotel in Addis Ababa demands that you remove another guest from the restaurant because they are 'too loud.' The other guest is at a normal volume. How do you handle this?

options.text: Immediately ask the other guest to quiet down since the VIP is more important
Tell the VIP that all guests are equal and you cannot do anything
Acknowledge the VIP's concern, offer to move them to a quieter table or private area, without disrupting the other guest
Ignore the request and hope they forget about it

questions.text: You work at an Ethio Telecom branch. Your supervisor has quietly told staff to 'make the porting process difficult' to retain customers. A customer wants to port their number. What should you do?

options.text: Follow your supervisor's instructions since they have authority
Process the porting request professionally and efficiently, as the customer has a legal right to port. If you have concerns about the instruction, raise it through proper channels
Refuse to process the request and say the system is down
Process it but make the customer wait unnecessarily long

questions.text: After resolving a difficult customer complaint at your bank, what is the most valuable next step?

options.text: Forget about it and move on to the next customer
Complain to your colleagues about how unreasonable the customer was
Document the issue and resolution, identify if it reveals a systemic problem, and suggest process improvements to prevent recurrence
Take a long break to recover from the stress

questions.text: A customer on the phone is on hold for 10 minutes while you research their issue. When you return, what is the best way to resume the conversation?

options.text: Jump straight into the solution without acknowledging the wait
Thank them sincerely for their patience, briefly explain what you were doing during the hold, and then present what you found
Apologize excessively for the next two minutes
Ask them to call back because you need more time

questions.text: A customer brings in a product for return that shows clear signs of misuse, but they insist it was defective from the start. There is no way to definitively prove either side. What is the best approach?

options.text: Accuse them of misuse and deny the return
Accept the return fully without question to avoid conflict
Acknowledge their concern without making accusations, explain what you observe, offer a fair compromise such as partial credit or exchange, and document the case
Tell them to come back with a witness who saw the defect

questions.text: You work at a ride-hailing call center in Addis Ababa. A female passenger calls reporting that her driver made inappropriate comments during the ride. What is your priority?

options.text: Ask her if she is sure she is not misunderstanding the driver's intentions
Take her report seriously, document every detail, assure her of follow-up action, offer a full refund for the ride, and escalate to the safety team immediately
Give her the driver's phone number so they can resolve it between themselves
Tell her to rate the driver poorly in the app and the system will handle it

questions.text: You notice that many customers at your bank branch struggle with the same form. It is poorly designed, but it has been used for years. What should you do?

options.text: Continue helping customers fill it out since that is your job
Proactively suggest a redesign to your manager with specific examples of customer confusion, while continuing to assist customers with the current form
Tell customers the form is confusing and blame the bank
Fill out the forms yourself on behalf of customers to save time

questions.text: A customer asks you a question and you realize mid-explanation that you gave them incorrect information. What should you do?

options.text: Continue with the incorrect information to avoid looking incompetent
Stop, honestly correct yourself, apologize for the confusion, and provide the accurate information
Finish your current explanation and hope they do not notice
Subtly change direction without acknowledging the error

questions.text: A customer demands to speak to your manager, but your manager is unavailable. The customer refuses to deal with anyone else. What is the best course of action?

options.text: Tell them the manager is not available and there is nothing you can do
Pretend to call the manager and relay messages yourself

Acknowledge their desire to speak with the manager, explain their unavailability, offer to document the issue in detail for the manager's urgent review, provide a specific callback timeframe, and ask if there is anything you can resolve now
Give them the manager's personal phone number

questions.text: You work at a mobile money agent kiosk. A customer wants to send a large amount of money but provides a recipient number that does not match the name they gave. What should you do?

options.text: Process the transaction since the customer knows what they want
Politely flag the discrepancy, ask the customer to verify the number, and explain that this check protects them from sending money to the wrong person
Refuse the transaction entirely
Process it but reduce the amount for safety

questions.text: A customer who was rude to you last week returns to your service counter. What is the correct professional approach?

options.text: Provide slow service as a subtle way to express your displeasure
Ask a colleague to serve them because you do not want to deal with them
Greet them warmly and serve them with the same professionalism as any other customer, without holding past interactions against them
Remind them about their previous behavior as a warning

questions.text: You need to inform a customer that a service they rely on is being discontinued. What is the best way to communicate this?

options.text: Send a generic SMS notification without any follow-up
Avoid telling them until the last day to prevent complaints
Deliver the news directly and empathetically, explain the reason, present alternative solutions that meet their needs, and offer assistance with the transition
Blame the decision on upper management so they do not get angry at you

questions.text: A customer has a confirmed booking but the flight was cancelled due to weather. They have an important business meeting and are extremely upset. What can you control?

options.text: Simply rebook them on the next available flight and move on

Empathize genuinely, explore every alternative including other airlines or routes, offer hotel and meal accommodations, help them communicate the delay to their meeting contacts if helpful, and ensure priority rebooking
Explain that weather cancellations are beyond your control and offer a refund
Suggest they should have planned for contingencies

questions.text: You work at a hotel front desk. A guest approaches you and confides that they feel unsafe because another guest has been following them. What is your immediate priority?

options.text: Tell them they are probably imagining it
Suggest they lock their door and avoid common areas
Take the concern seriously immediately, move them to a safe area, alert hotel security, offer to change their room, and document the incident while keeping them informed of actions taken
Confront the other guest yourself about the behavior

questions.text: Your company has just launched a new policy that you personally disagree with. A customer asks you about it. What is the appropriate response?

options.text: Share your personal opinion so the customer knows you are on their side
Explain the policy accurately and professionally, focusing on any benefits to the customer, while keeping personal opinions private
Tell the customer you disagree with the policy and suggest they complain to management
Avoid discussing the policy and change the subject

questions.text: A customer at your bank wants to open a business account. During the conversation, they mention they are also looking for a loan. You handle accounts but not loans. What is the best approach?

options.text: Ignore the loan mention and focus only on the account opening
Try to handle the loan inquiry yourself to provide one-stop service
Complete the account opening, acknowledge their loan interest, and personally introduce them to the loans officer with a brief summary so they do not have to repeat themselves
Give them the loans department phone number and let them figure it out

questions.text: A customer has been escalated to you after two previous agents failed to resolve their billing dispute. They are exhausted and distrustful. What is the most effective way to begin the interaction?

options.text: Start fresh and ask them to explain everything from the beginning

Review the previous interaction notes first, then introduce yourself, summarize what you already know so they see you have done your homework, acknowledge their frustration with the process, and commit to owning the resolution personally
Apologize for your colleagues and promise you are better than them
Immediately offer a credit to end the conversation quickly
